title: Audit next-row PitStop as a sequence-leak feature
summary: The label is PitNextLap while the data already contains PitStop for each row.
rationale: The label is PitNextLap while the data already contains PitStop for each row. If rows form complete driver-race lap sequences, shifting PitStop by -1 within each sequence may nearly reconstruct the target; at minimum it is the most important validation-trap check.
implementation_hint: Sort by Race_Year or Race+Year, Driver, LapNumber, id; create Lead1_PitStop, Lead1_TyreLifeReset, and NextRowSameDriverRace flags. Test Lead1_PitStop alone on train under race-held-out CV before blending it with model probabilities.
expected_effect: Potentially very large AUC lift if the public test exposes full sequences; otherwise it identifies and prevents misleading CV from future-information features.
risk: May be considered non-causal leakage if the intended prediction setting is live lap-by-lap; sequence keys can be ambiguous when laps are missing or testing data is mixed in.
